title: Standing in your own hallway, keys in hand
subtitle: A short guided visualisation for making the moment of moving into your own home feel familiar before it happens.
excerpt: A visualisation practice from Day 25 of the MONEY program. Use to rehearse the moment of walking through the front door of a property you own for the first time.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 25 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from Day 25 of the MONEY program. The scene is the first moment of ownership: the front door, the key, the hallway. Nothing dramatic happens in this visualisation. That's the point, the goal is to make ownership feel ordinary.
Sit quietly. Close your eyes. Take two slow breaths.
Picture yourself standing outside the front door of a property you own. It doesn't need to be perfect or specific, just a door on a quiet street, yours.
You have the keys. Feel the weight of them in your hand, the cool metal, the ring. You put the key in the lock. The door opens.
Step into the hallway. Look straight ahead. Notice the floor under your feet. The light coming in through a window at the end of the hall, or from above the door behind you. The house is quiet.
Stand there for a moment. You are inside a building that is yours. Not a rental, not a viewing, yours. Say it quietly to yourself: mine.
Let your body settle into the scene. There is no urgency. You are not about to leave. When you are ready, open your eyes.
Adapted from Day 25 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
